title: sisymbrium officinale subsp. leiocarpum
intro: Sisymbrium officinale subsp. leiocarpum, also known as the smallseed hemlock, is a subspecies of the Sisymbrium officinale plant, a member of the mustard family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Sisymbrium officinale subsp. leiocarpum include relieving coughs, reducing fever, and soothing sore throats. Herbal preparations of the plant may be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
